channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Discord accused of exposing kids to graphic, violent content in lawsuit
item.description: New Jersey Attorney General Matthew Platkin filed a lawsuit against Discord on Thursday, alleging the popular messaging app misled parents about its safety features.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Senate Democrat presses Noem on Real ID deadline
item.description: Sen. Jack Reed (D-R.I.) pressed Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on the Real ID requirements being implemented in two weeks. The new measure requires individuals to provide a U.S. birth certificate or a U.S. passport, or a permanent resident card (green card) if they are a non-U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump administration replaces federal COVID-19 response page with 'lab leak' explainer
item.description: The Trump White House on Friday replaced the covid.gov website that provided resources and information on COVID-19 with a webpage promoting the theory that the pandemic was a result of a laboratory leak in Wuhan, China, which a House GOP panel strongly favored in a report released last year.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Star Wars Celebration is in full swing, and Lucasfilm just dropped more details on its Beyond Victory experience for Meta Quest, and I couldn't be more stoked
item.description: If you're in Japan for Celebration, be sure to play the Beyond Victory experience for Meta Quest demo.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: The lawsuit claimed Facebook was using cookies to collect data on users in 2010 and 2011, even when they were logged out or visiting other websites.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Meta is set to train its AI models with Europeans' public data, and you can stop it doing so
item.description: The Big Tech giant resumes its AI training plan, after pausing the launch of Meta AI amid EU data regulators' concerns.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: SpaceX is the top contender to build Trump’s ‘Golden Dome’ missile shield, sources say
item.description: Elon Musk’s SpaceX and two partners have emerged as frontrunners to win a crucial part of President Donald Trump’s “Golden Dome” missile defense shield, six people familiar with the matter said.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: 7 simple ways to protect your credit cards while traveling
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson shares several easy ways to keep your credit cards safe from digital thieves while you're traveling this summer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A restaurant in Havana defies Cuba's tech struggles with digital menus and a robot waitress
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — For Sonia Pérez, stepping into Havana’s Doña Alicia restaurant felt like entering a movie.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Users receive unprovoked Windows 11 offers after Intune code glitch
item.description: Enterprise users are receiving unprovoked offers to upgrade to Windows 11 due to an issue in Microsoft’s device management tools. The issue is with Microsoft’s Intune software, which typically allows system administrators to manage mobile devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok starts testing Footnotes, a new feature that looks a lot like X’s Community Notes
item.description: TikTok is launching its own version of community notes on the platform, called “Footnotes.” The crowd-sourced approach to moderation, where users add additional context to posts, has become increasingly popular on social media networks after X (then called Twitter) first launched it in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Zuckerberg once floated spinning off Instagram over antitrust fears, email reveals in trial
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Text scams cost consumers $470M in 2024: FTC
item.description: Most commonly, the FTC said these scams started with a text message, specifically ones about issues with a fictitious package delivery.
item.dc:creator: Drew Miller, NewsNation

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nvidia expects ban on chip exports to China to cost $5.5B
item.description: Nvidia now expects new US government restrictions on exports of its H20 chip to China will cost the company as much as $5.5 billion, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CVE funding cuts reversed after security concerns raised
item.description: Editor’s note: After this story was published earlier today, CISA signed a contract extension that averts a shutdown of the MITRE CVE program.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: AI system restores speech for paralyzed patients using own voice
item.description: Developed at two California universities, the innovative technology combines brain-computer interfaces with advanced AI to decode neural activity into audible speech.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft at 50: The 7 biggest game-changers through five decades
item.description: When Microsoft was founded 50 years ago this month, it wasn’t clear the company would last 50 weeks, much less 50 years.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The programming language wars
item.description: Microsoft recently decided to rewrite the TypeScript tool chain using Go. This has caused a stir as folks wonder why Microsoft chose the Go language instead of their own C# or even TypeScript itself. I have to say that it is a curious choice.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Zuckerberg suggested spinning Instagram off into separate company
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg previously considered spinning off Instagram into a separate company in the face of growing antitrust concerns and competing interests with Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: JRuby 10 brings faster startup times
item.description: JRuby 10, the latest release of the Ruby language variant built atop the JVM, has arrived, bringing startup time improvements, support for Java 21, and compatibility with Ruby 3.4. Release of JRuby 10 was announced April 14. JRuby 10 can be downloaded from jruby.org.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Cato Networks augments CASB with genAI security
item.description: Cato Networks recently unveiled new generative AI capabilities in its Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that the secure access service edge (SASE) provider says will let enterprise IT organizations detect, analyze, and gain insights into the use of genAI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google’s bold step toward hybrid AI integration
item.description: News stories about cloud services and artificial intelligence are generally predictable. Another generative AI model, another upgraded cloud feature, another boast about “leading the market in innovation.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The network blueprint to take your modern enterprise global
item.description: Adopting new technologies is synonymous with improving productivity, enhancing decision-making, and driving innovations today. Almost every enterprise integrates some form of AI/ML capabilities within their operations or tapping into the Internet of Things (IoT) to keep operations at the edge.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
